title: Russia to Increase Economic Influence in Syria Through Commercial Logistics Hub at Syrian Port
imageAlt: Russia to Increase Economic Influence in Syria Through Commercial Logistics Hub at Syrian Port
keyPoints: Russia to Increase Economic Influence in Syria Through Commercial Logistics Hub at Syrian Port
whyMatters: Noticia de actualidad en el sector de internacional.

multimedia.alt: Russia to Increase Economic Influence in Syria Through Commercial Logistics Hub at Syrian Port
multimedia.caption: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney speaks during a media availability in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, Thursday, July 9, 2026. (Adrian Wyld/The Canadian Press via AP)

multimedia.alt: Russia to Increase Economic Influence in Syria Through Commercial Logistics Hub at Syrian Port
multimedia.caption: Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Saudi Crown Prince and Prime Minister, welcomes Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney in Jeddah on Thursday. (SPA)

fullArticle:
Moscow is moving to consolidate its economic influence in Syria by establishing a commercial logistics hub in the port of Tartous, a move that reflects a shift in its strategy from military presence to expanded economic weight.

The Russian move coincides with discussions over the future of Moscow’s military bases at Tartous and Hmeimim, as Washington plans to renew contracts and investments in the country.

According to Reuters, Russia hopes by mid-July to have the commercial logistics hub up and running in one of two berths at the naval base it leases in the Tartous port, while keeping a military presence at the other.
